title: Pods fail to start due to "Error: creating container storage: the container name "pod_name-pod-infra" is already in use" after every host reboot
body:
### Issue Description

Every time I reboot the Podman host, all pods fail to start due to a "pod_name-pod-infra" container name already being used by some other container. Single containers (those not in a pod) start fine.

The container that apparently uses the name however does not seem to exist. I can't inspect it by its id and it does not show up in `podman ps --all` either. It does show up in containers.json file though (in fact, the containers.json file contains references only to the "pod_name-pod-infra" containers for all of my pods).

I can remove the pod-infra containers manually with `podman container rm <container_id>` and the pods start fine afterwards but that's not a viable solution since I have to do it for each pod after every reboot.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create a pod with containers and start it
2. Reboot and wait
3. Check the pod unit's logs


### Describe the results you received

Pods fail to start after host reboot. Example logs can be found below.

**Journalctl logs:**
```
Starting xyz-pod.service

2025-12-17 02:46:22.750216607 +0100 CET m=+0.048970114 pod remove e6333a255ab2219f3a1819033a121c6b6eab784f22a25aaf11c527ccba5a7052 (image=, name=xyz-pod)

Error: creating container storage: the container name "xyz-pod-infra" is already in use by 75ae2d3e7490fae224ec8de3218be25117c13c277a8a7a74216754144a3c1813. You have to remove that container to be able to reuse that name: that name is already in use by an external entity, or use --replace to instruct Podman to do so.

xyz-pod.service: Control process exited, code=exited, status=125/n/a

xyz-pod.service: Failed with result 'exit-code'.

Failed to start xyz-pod.service
```

**Containers.json reference:**
```
{
"id": "75ae2d3e7490fae224ec8de3218be25117c13c277a8a7a74216754144a3c1813",
"names": ["xyz-pod-infra"],
"image": "",
"layer": "14452a4093ae8d6018e117d46fa89c1902ab07be1a37c0a6e6daca7bcc2faf12",
"metadata": "{\"image-name\":\"\",\"image-id\":\"\",\"name\":\"xyz-pod-infra\",\"created-at\":1765934003}",
"created": "2025-12-17T01:13:28.164188969Z",
"uidmap": [{ "container_id": 0, "host_id": 7169, "size": 1024 }],
"gidmap": [{ "container_id": 0, "host_id": 7169, "size": 1024 }],
"flags": {
"MountLabel": "system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c107,c1014",
"ProcessLabel": "system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c107,c1014"
}
}
```

### Describe the results you expected

The pod and all its containers should start fine after reboot.

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Additional environment details

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I've got a new Podman service deployment and started running into this as well. Bare metal machine. 3 other pods start fine but one pod cannot start due to this error.

If I look for the offending container, it's not listed for me to remove.
I have the same issue. Using rootles Podman v5.7.1 on baremetal.
If you `podman rm --force` the name of the container, does it solve the issue?
